problem: The app-local surface-particle implementation mixes reusable particle state and deterministic update concepts with Spatial panel carriers and private payload hooks.
owns: Particle topology and bounded state descriptors independent of the app shell.
Deterministic CPU reference update fixtures and damaged-input expectations.
Neutral driver-bank inputs with no application semantics.
does_not_own: Spatial SDK panels, Android surfaces, Vulkan WSI, or JNI lifecycle.
Private shader payloads, effect formulas, tuned profiles, or study behavior.
Quest packaging, permissions, or runtime feature activation.
app_specific_exclusions: Surface target names and questionnaire workflow.
Private icosphere payload and marker vocabulary.
Spatial panel placement, recenter controls, and app-specific presentation.

provenance.source: Rusty Quest Spatial Camera Panel app-local surface-particle modules
provenance.lesson: Separate deterministic particle contracts from the platform carrier and application workflow.
provenance.rejected_overreach: Do not copy Spatial SDK, JNI, Vulkan WSI, or private downstream payload behavior into Matter.

consumers.evidence: Concrete MOD-003 hypothesis: apps/native-renderer-android consumes the same neutral Matter snapshot/render-payload conformance fixture through its separate OpenXR/Vulkan private-particle slot.

evidence: SpatialSurfaceParticleRouteModule.kt keeps the route explicit opt-in.
native-receipt host tests cover the current surface-particle boundary.
The workflow feature lock disables the candidate by default.
Scoped graph S:/Work/migration/coding-graph/morphospace-mod001-particle-20260711 maps the Matter, Lattice, Optics, and Quest pressure surfaces.
Existing Matter surface-runtime and particle schema IDs are the contract baseline; no parallel app-derived schema is justified.

rollback.strategy: Keep the existing app-local implementation and remove only the candidate/extraction adapter if parity fails.
rollback.acceptance: The app static and native receipt tests return to their pre-extraction results with the feature still explicit opt-in.
